senses.gloss: [with noun or pronoun ‘how is it different’, along with مِن (min, + noun or pronoun) ‘from something else’] (usually implying there is no relationship)

examples.english: What does this have to do with that? / What comparison is there between them?!
How far is the soil from the Pleiades!

examples.text: الحملات العامة تجلب الناس المحاذين للخطر إلى مراكز مثل مركز المراقبة بي.سي.أن ، أين^([sic]) يمكنهم إجراء إختبار^([sic]) على فيروس فقدان المناعة بطريقة سريعة ومجهولة

etymology.text:
From the root ء ي ن (ʔ y n), meaning “to come or approach”, “to become or happen”, “to draw near to a place or time”; related to ء ي ي (ʔ y y) and آن (ʔān).

Causative based on the root ء ي ن (ʔ y n) extracted from أَيُون (ʔayūn, “ion”).
